steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student claims Ecuador has ‘several different types of volcanoes.’ To support this, the evidence must show multiple distinct volcano types from the table's ‘Volcano type’ column — not eruption dates or any detail that groups volcanoes into the same type.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Wolf is a shield volcano, Tungurahua is a stratovolcano, Imbabura is a compound volcano, and Chacana is a caldera volcano.’: Lists four volcanoes with four distinct types, directly proving the claim of type diversity. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Wolf, Tungurahua, and Imbabura are all stratovolcanoes, whereas Chacana is the only caldera volcano.’: Contradicts the table (Wolf is a shield volcano, not a stratovolcano) and misrepresents the diversity. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Wolf, Tungurahua, Imbabura, and Chacana each most recently erupted in different years.’: Addresses eruption years, not volcano types — irrelevant to the student's claim. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Wolf and Tungurahua are both shield volcanoes, whereas Imbabura and Chacana are both compound volcanoes.’: Contradicts the table and reduces the apparent diversity to only two types. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
